message:
Add onboarding flow and improve UI components

- Add onboarding components and useOnboarding hook
- Update Providers, SwipeablePromptCard components
- Fix color consistency tests
- Add admin, changelog, discovery e2e tests
- Update documentation files

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expanded EXISTING_JFP_STRUCTURE.md with full command/behavior spec; updated PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md to leverage /dp libs; updated PLAN_TO_PORT_JFP_TO_RUST.md with library list; updated FEATURE_PARITY.md to include config + interactive. Awaiting reassignment from VioletHill.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"VioletHill","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:10:58.492607101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T03:55:15.983775449Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-02u","title":"Build ChangelogAccordion for prompt detail pages","description":"**Details:** - Shows version history when prompt.changelog exists - Accordion expansion (collapsed by default) - Lists changes chronologically (newest first) - Version, date, and change description per entry **Changelog Type (in types.ts):** ```typescript interface PromptChange { version: string; date: string; changes: string[]; } // On Prompt interface: changelog?: PromptChange[]; ``` **Implementation:** ```typescript // apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx interface ChangelogAccordionProps { changelog: PromptChange[]; } export function ChangelogAccordion({ changelog }: ChangelogAccordionProps) { const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false); if (!changelog?.length) return null; return (
{expanded && (
    {changelog.map((entry) => (
  • {entry.version} {entry.date}
      {entry.changes.map((change, i) => (
    • {change}
      • ))}
    • ))}
)}
); } ``` **Location:** apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx **Reasoning:** Changelogs build trust and help users understand prompt evolution. Accordion keeps the UI clean while making history accessible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:35:24.004177827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","close_reason":"Created ChangelogAccordion with collapsible UI, type-specific icons/colors, and timeline styling.","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-06hp","title":"Task: Generate combined coverage report","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:49:56.733833988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:48:14.075783682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:48:14.075783682Z","close_reason":"Implemented merge-coverage.ts script, added coverage:all and coverage:merge scripts to package.json, updated CI to generate and upload combined coverage report","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-06hp","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vyjo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:57:08.032982405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-08e","title":"Implement jfp list command -- Details: Lists all prompts. Filters: --category, --tag, --featured, --limit. Human output: compact table with ID, category, tags, description. JSON output: array of prompt metadata. Apply Cache-Control headers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:27:17.955931087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:24:25.511928331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:24:25.511928331Z","close_reason":"Completed as part of Next.js app and core package setup. Barrel exports, root layout, main page, and API routes are all functional.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-2vj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:37:57.166213321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-30k9","title":"Build marketing landing page","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-6ax. ## Task Build the marketing landing page for organic discovery. ## Page: / (root) ### Hero Section - Large headline: "The Prompt Library for AI Power Users" - Subheadline: "Discover, create, and share prompts for Claude, GPT, and beyond." - Primary CTA: "Get Started Free" or "Try Premium" - Secondary: "Explore Prompts" - Hero visual: Screenshot or animation of product ### Features Grid 6-8 feature cards: 1. Swap Meet - Community marketplace 2. Prompt Packs - Curated bundles 3. Skills - AI agent configurations 4. Collections - Organize your library 5. CLI Integration - Terminal access 6. Analytics - Track performance ### How It Works Three-step visual: 1. Sign up with Google (instant) 2. Browse or create prompts 3. Share and track usage ### Testimonials (placeholder) - Quote cards (can be placeholder initially) - Profile photos and names ### Pricing Preview - Side-by-side Free vs Premium - "View full pricing" link ### Final CTA - "Ready to level up your AI workflow?" - Sign up button ### Footer - Product links (Features, Pricing, Docs) - Legal links (Terms, Privacy, Guidelines) - Social links - Copyright ## Implementation - Server component for SEO - Animations with Framer Motion - Responsive grid layouts - Lazy load images - Focus on Core Web Vitals ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:18:40.074852074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T09:10:28.769413705Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T09:10:28.769413705Z","close_reason":"Implemented marketing landing page with Features, How It Works, Testimonials, Pricing Preview, and Final CTA sections. Updated Footer with all required links. All quality gates pass.","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-32fx","title":"Implement public roadmap and feature voting","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-zuri. ## Task Create a public roadmap with feature voting to engage community. ## Why This Matters - Transparency about direction - Community input on priorities - Reduce duplicate feature requests - Build anticipation ## Features ### 1. Public Roadmap - Planned features list - Current development status - Recently shipped features - Timeline (quarters, not dates) ### 2. Feature Voting - Upvote features - Add comments - Submit new ideas - Track your submissions ### 3. Status Categories | Status | Description | |--------|-------------| | Under Review | New submission | | Planned | Will be built | | In Progress | Currently building | | Shipped | Live in production | | Declined | Won't build (with reason) | ### 4. User Submissions - Submit feature idea - Add description - Add use case - Similar feature detection ## Database Schema ```typescript export const featureRequests = pgTable('feature_requests', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), // Content title: text('title').notNull(), description: text('description').notNull(), useCase: text('use_case'), // Status status: text('status').default('under_review'), statusNote: text('status_note'), // Tracking submittedBy: uuid('submitted_by').references(() => userProfiles.id), voteCount: integer('vote_count').default(0), commentCount: integer('comment_count').default(0), // Timeline plannedQuarter: text('planned_quarter'), // e.g., 'Q2 2026' shippedAt: timestamp('shipped_at'), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }); export const featureVotes = pgTable('feature_votes', { featureId: uuid('feature_id').references(() => featureRequests.id).notNull(), userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ primaryKey({ columns: [table.featureId, table.userId] }), ]); ``` ## Public Pages - /roadmap - Main roadmap view - /roadmap/[id] - Feature detail - /roadmap/submit - Submit idea ## Admin Features - Change feature status - Set planned quarter - Merge duplicates - Respond to requests ## Notifications - Status change notification - Shipped notification - Vote milestone (10, 50, 100) ## Files to Create - src/app/(public)/roadmap/page.tsx - src/app/(public)/roadmap/[id]/page.tsx - src/app/api/roadmap/route.ts - src/app/(app)/admin/roadmap/page.tsx - src/components/roadmap/ ## Success Criteria - Roadmap publicly accessible - Voting works correctly - Submissions moderated - Status updates communicated ","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:15.104473957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:15.104473957Z","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-38vm","title":"E2E Tests: Changelog and What's New","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ldzr. ## Task Create E2E tests for the changelog and what's new features. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Changelog Page** - Changelog entries display - Version grouping works - Date formatting correct - Links to related content 2. **What's New Modal** - Shows for new features - Dismisses correctly - Don't show again works - Images/GIFs display 3. **Feature Announcements** - Banner for major features - Targetted to relevant users - Dismiss persists - Links work 4. **RSS/Atom Feed** - Feed URL accessible - Feed content valid - New entries appear ## Logging Requirements - Log changelog views - Log what's new interactions - Log banner dismissals - Screenshot modal states ## Files to Create - e2e/changelog/page.spec.ts - e2e/changelog/whats-new.spec.ts - e2e/changelog/announcements.spec.ts ## Success Criteria - Changelog displays correctly - What's new helpful - Users stay informed - Not intrusive ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:28.449971001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:28.449971001Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-38vm","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:34.506342812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-38vm","title":"E2E Tests: Changelog and What's New","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ldzr. ## Task Create E2E tests for the changelog and what's new features. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Changelog Page** - tests mobile viewport shows correct layout, touch-friendly buttons, and bottom sheet behavior on 390x844 viewport with hasTouch enabled","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3968","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:58:11.341337517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","title":"Task: Create TestLogger class with levels (debug/info/step/error)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:46:26.173743647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:22:20.532200200Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:22:20.532200200Z","close_reason":"Created TestLogger class with debug/info/step/error levels, timestamps, context formatting, and file output for CI artifacts","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-50cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:54:52.182623225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3fms","title":"Add visual separation between Featured and Browse sections","description":"# Task: Clarify Featured vs Browse Section Boundary ## Problem Statement Currently, the Featured section appears immediately after the Hero, followed by the filter controls and browse grid. This creates confusion because: 1. **Filters don't affect featured**: User applies a filter, sees 6 featured prompts unchanged, thinks filtering is broken 2. **No visual boundary**: Featured and Browse blend together 3. **Duplicated prompts**: Featured prompts may appear again in the browse grid ## Current Layout ``` Hero (search + category pills) ↓ Featured Prompts (6 cards, unaffected by filters) ↓ Filter Controls (Category, Tags, Clear) ↓ Results Header ("All Prompts" / "Search Results") ↓ Browse Grid (filtered results) ``` ## Proposed Solution ### Option A: Move Featured Above Filters, Add Clear Separator ```tsx {/* Hero */} {/* Featured Section - clearly labeled, visually distinct */}

Featured Prompts

Curated by the team
{/* Clear visual break */}
{/* Browse Section - with filters */}

Browse All Prompts

``` ### Option B: Integrate Featured into Results Remove separate featured section. Instead: - Add "Featured" badge to featured prompts in the grid - Sort featured prompts to top by default - Filters affect all prompts including featured ```tsx // In PromptGrid or page.tsx const sortedPrompts = useMemo(() => { // Featured first, then the rest const featured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => p.featured); const nonFeatured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => !p.featured); return [...featured, ...nonFeatured]; }, [filteredPrompts]); ``` ### Option C: Collapsible Featured Section (Best of Both) Show featured by default, allow collapsing: ```tsx const [showFeatured, setShowFeatured] = useState(true);
Featured Prompts {featuredCount}
``` ## Recommended Approach **Option A** is simplest and clearest: - Distinct visual sections with clear labels - Gradient separator creates elegant break - "Curated by the team" explains why they're special - Users understand filters apply only to "Browse" section ## Implementation Details 1. Add section wrapper with border-bottom to FeaturedPromptsSection 2. Add "Browse All Prompts" heading above filter controls 3. Add subtle gradient divider between sections 4. Consider adding "(not affected by filters)" note near Featured heading ## Testing 1. Apply category filter - featured section unchanged, browse section filtered 2. Verify visual separation is clear in both light/dark modes 3. Screen reader: sections are announced as distinct regions 4. Mobile: sections stack correctly ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx (possibly) ## Dependencies - Should be done after color consistency (page.tsx) - Affects layout, may need responsive testing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:47:27.281567949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T07:46:09.758005579Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T07:46:09.758005579Z","close_reason":"Implemented Option A: Added 'Featured Prompts' title with 'Curated by the team' subtitle, border-bottom on Featured section, gradient divider between sections, 'Browse All Prompts' section heading, and contextual results count. Build passes.","compaction_level":0} @@ -419,14 +419,14 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-y4l","title":"Implement jfp serve MCP server -- Details: MCP server over stdio. Resource: prompt:// returns prompt content. Tools: search_prompts (query, category, tags, limit), render_prompt (id, variables, context). Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Optional self-update flow (disabled by default). Check weekly or 'jfp update-cli' on demand. Download from GitHub releases. Verify checksum (SHA256SUMS.txt). Atomic replace: download → verify → rename. Rollback on failure: keep jfp.bak, restore if new binary fails exec. Respects updates.autoUpdate config setting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:46:33.288029788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:22.758720145Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:22.758720145Z","close_reason":"Implemented checksum verification, atomic replace + rollback, and update check tracking in update-cli","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-y60","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:49:25.771789256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-y69a","title":"E2E Tests: Staff Picks and Featured Content","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-i53g. ## Task Create E2E tests for staff picks and featured content functionality. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Staff Picks Display** - Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create health check endpoints for monitoring and load balancers. ## Why This Matters - Load balancer health checks - Kubernetes liveness/readiness - Uptime monitoring integration - Quick service status ## Endpoints ### 1. Basic Health (/health) - Returns 200 if server running - No external dependencies - Used by load balancers - Ultra-fast response ### 2. Ready Check (/health/ready) - Checks database connection - Checks Redis (if used) - Checks critical services - Returns 503 if not ready ### 3. Detailed Status (/health/status) - Full system status - Individual service status - Version information - Protected (admin only) ## Implementation ### Basic Health ```typescript // src/app/api/health/route.ts export async function GET() { return Response.json({ status: 'ok', timestamp: Date.now() }); } ``` ### Ready Check ```typescript // src/app/api/health/ready/route.ts export async function GET() { const checks = await Promise.allSettled([ checkDatabase(), checkSupabase(), checkStripe(), ]); const results = { database: checks[0].status === 'fulfilled', supabase: checks[1].status === 'fulfilled', stripe: checks[2].status === 'fulfilled', }; const allHealthy = Object.values(results).every(Boolean); return Response.json( { status: allHealthy ? 'ready' : 'degraded', checks: results }, { status: allHealthy ? 200 : 503 } ); } async function checkDatabase() { const result = await db.execute(sql`SELECT 1`); if (!result) throw new Error('Database unreachable'); } ``` ### Detailed Status (Admin) ```typescript // src/app/api/health/status/route.ts export async function GET(request: Request) { // Require admin auth const user = await authenticateRequest(request); if (!user?.isAdmin) return Response.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 }); return Response.json({ version: process.env.APP_VERSION, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, uptime: process.uptime(), memory: process.memoryUsage(), services: { database: await getDatabaseStatus(), supabase: await getSupabaseStatus(), stripe: await getStripeStatus(), }, features: getFeatureFlags(), }); } ``` ## Monitoring Integration - Configure Vercel health checks - Set up Better Uptime/Checkly - PagerDuty alerting ## Files to Create - src/app/api/health/route.ts - src/app/api/health/ready/route.ts - src/app/api/health/status/route.ts - src/lib/health/checks.ts ## Success Criteria - /health returns 200 quickly - /health/ready checks dependencies - Load balancer compatible - Monitoring integrated ## Hardening Additions - /health is dependency-free and cache-disabled. - /health/ready should avoid leaking internals; return booleans only. - /health/status remains admin-only; strip secrets. - Add lightweight integration tests for status codes and JSON shape. +## 1) Entry Points and CLI Framework
+- Entry: `jfp.ts` -> calls `checkForUpdatesInBackground()` then `cli.parse()` from `packages/cli/src/index.ts`.
+- CLI framework: `cac`.
+- Global flag: `--no-color` is handled BEFORE chalk import. If `--no-color` OR `NO_COLOR` OR `JFP_NO_COLOR` is set, it sets `process.env.NO_COLOR = "1"`.

-## 2) Output and Error Conventions
-- **JSON output** if `--json` OR `!process.stdout.isTTY`.

-- Many commands use `process.exit(1)` on errors; no global error mapping.
+## 2) Global Output Rules and Exit Codes
+- JSON output rule: `shouldOutputJson(options)` returns true if `options.json === true` OR `!process.stdout.isTTY`.
+- Color disable: `--no-color`, `NO_COLOR`, or `JFP_NO_COLOR`.
+- Exit codes observed:
+  - `1` for most errors
+  - `2` for invalid skill IDs in `uninstall`
+  - `130` for user-cancel (Ctrl+C) in interactive variable fill
+- Error payload patterns are per-command (no single global schema). Some commands have stable shapes (notably `show` not_found).

+- `config list`: prints full config; JSON uses `loadConfig()`.
+- `config get <key>`: dot notation lookup; error `not_found` if missing.

+- `config reset`: overwrites with defaults.

+## 4) Credentials and Auth

+### Storage
+- Path: `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME|HOME}/jfp/credentials.json`.
+- Directory perms: `0700` (mkdir with mode).
+- File perms: `0600` (writeFile with mode).
+- Atomic write via temp file + rename.
+
+### Expiry and Refresh
+- Expiry check uses 5-minute buffer.
+- Refresh endpoint: `${JFP_PREMIUM_URL || https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com}/api/cli/token/refresh`.
+- Refresh body: `{ refresh_token, client_id: "jfp-cli" }`.
+- If refresh succeeds, saves new credentials.

+### Env Override
+- `JFP_TOKEN` bypasses credentials file and supplies token directly (no user info).
 
 ## 5) API Client
 File: `packages/cli/src/lib/api-client.ts`
 
 - Base URL: `JFP_PREMIUM_API_URL` or `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com/api`.
-- Timeout: 30s default.
-- Adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>` if available.
-- Parses JSON only when `Content-Type` includes `application/json`.
-- Error message uses `error` or `message` field; falls back to statusText.
+- Timeout: 30s (abort controller).
+- Headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `Authorization: Bearer <token>` if token.
+- JSON parsing only when `Content-Type` includes `application/json`.
+- Error message extraction: use `error` or `message` string fields from JSON, else `statusText`.
+- ApiResponse shape: `{ ok, status, data?, error? }`.
+- Helpers: `isAuthError` (401), `isPermissionError` (403), `isNotFoundError` (404), `requiresPremium` (403 + error contains "premium").
 
-## 6) Registry Loader
+## 6) Registry Loader (SWR)
 File: `packages/cli/src/lib/registry-loader.ts`
 
-- **SWR** pattern:
-  - If cache exists: return cached immediately.
-  - If stale and `autoRefresh=true`, refresh in background.
-  - If no cache: fetch remote; fall back to bundled prompts.
-- **ETag** support with `If-None-Match`.
-- **Cache freshness**: `cacheTtl` seconds.
-- **Local prompts**: if enabled, read JSON files from `localPrompts.dir`.
-  - Accepts either a single prompt object or array of prompts per file.
-  - Zod validation; warns if invalid but has `id`.
-- **Offline prompts**: from `library/prompts.json` (see Offline Library).
-- Merge order: offline → cached/remote → local.
-
-## 7) Offline Library (current JSON storage)
+### Cache
+- `registry.json` and `registry.meta.json` with `RegistryMeta`:
+  - `version`, `etag`, `fetchedAt`, `promptCount`.
+- Uses ETag with `If-None-Match`.
+- Cache TTL in seconds from config.
+
+### Sources and Merge Order
+- Local prompts (`~/.config/jfp/local/*.json`) if enabled.
+- Offline prompts from `~/.config/jfp/library/prompts.json` (saved prompts).
+- Cache/remote/bundled prompts from `@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts`.
+- Merge order: offline -> cached/remote/bundled -> local (later wins by id).
+
+### Local Prompts Validation
+- Accepts JSON file with single prompt or array of prompts.
+- Validates with `PromptSchema`.
+- Warns if invalid but has `id` key.
+
+### SWR Behavior
+- If cache exists and stale and `autoRefresh`, triggers background refresh.
+- If no cache, tries remote fetch; if fails, falls back to bundled prompts.
+
+## 7) Offline Library (JSON Cache)
 File: `packages/cli/src/lib/offline.ts`
 
 ### Paths
-- Library dir: `~/.config/jfp/library`
-- Prompts file: `~/.config/jfp/library/prompts.json`
-- Sync meta: `~/.config/jfp/library/sync.meta.json`
+- Library dir: `~/.config/jfp/library`.
+- Prompts file: `prompts.json`.

-SyncedPrompt:

-## 10) Validations / Default Values
-- `search --limit`: must be positive number; else exit 1.
-- Skill/bundle id safety: `isSafeSkillId` regex.
-- `show`: not found -> exit 1 + JSON error `{"error":"not_found"}`.

-## 11) SQL Queries
-- **None** (current CLI uses JSON files and HTTP APIs only).
+- Manifest: `manifest.json` in skills dir.
+- `FullSkillManifest`: `{ generatedAt, jfpVersion, entries[] }`.
+- Entry fields: `{ id, kind, version, hash, updatedAt }`.
+- JFP-generated detection: `x_jfp_generated: true` in YAML frontmatter.
+- Hash: `computeSkillHash` from `@jeffreysprompts/core/export`.
+- `checkSkillModification`:
+  - `canOverwrite` true if file does not exist OR (generated AND not modified).
+  - If manifest entry missing but file exists, treated as modified.
+
+## 9) Variable Handling
+File: `packages/cli/src/lib/variables.ts`
+
+- CLI variable flags: `--VAR=value` where `VAR` must start with A-Z or a-z, then alnum/underscore.
+- File variable max size: 100KB; if larger, read first 100KB and append truncation notice.
+- Prompting uses `@inquirer/prompts` for select, multiline, file, path, text.
+- For `file` type: reads file content. For `path` type: passes raw value.
+- Dynamic defaults from `getDynamicDefaults(process.cwd())` (CWD, PROJECT_NAME).
+
+## 10) Commands and Behavior
+
+### list
+- Options: `--category`, `--tag`, `--mine`, `--saved`, `--json`.
+- If `--mine` or `--saved` and not logged in -> `not_authenticated` error, exit 1.
+- If logged in:
+  - With `--mine` and/or `--saved`, calls:
+    - `GET /cli/prompts/mine` and/or `GET /cli/prompts/saved`.
+  - Without these flags, tries `GET /cli/prompts/mine` with allowFailure and merges into public prompts.
+- Offline fallback: if network error and offline library exists, uses offline saved prompts and sets `offline` output.
+- JSON output: `{ prompts, count, offline?, offlineAge? }`.
+
+### search
+- Options: `--limit` (default 10), `--mine`, `--saved`, `--all`, `--local`, `--json`.
+- Invalid `--limit` -> `invalid_limit` error, exit 1.
+- If `--mine/--saved/--all` and not logged in -> `not_authenticated` error, exit 1.
+- If logged in but not premium and `--mine/--saved/--all` -> `premium_required` error, exit 1.
+- Local search uses BM25 index from core (`buildIndex`, `searchPrompts`).
+- Personal search:
+  - Endpoint: `/cli/search/mine` (mine only), `/cli/search/saved` (saved only), otherwise `/cli/search`.
+  - If 401 -> `auth_expired`.
+  - On network error and offline library exists -> offline search (saved prompts).
+  - NOTE: code calls `response.json()` on ApiResponse; as written this will throw (bug).
+- Merge: personal results + local results, dedupe by id, sort by score desc, limit.
+- JSON output: `{ results, query, authenticated, offline?, warning? }`.
+
+### show
+- Options: `--json`, `--raw`.
+- Not found: JSON payload is exactly `{ "error": "not_found" }` (no message), exit 1.
+- `--raw` prints `prompt.content`.
+
+### copy
+- Options: `--fill`, `--json`.
+- Variable handling: parse `--VAR=value`, apply dynamic defaults, process file/path variables.
+- `--fill` prompts for missing variables; Ctrl+C -> error `cancelled`, exit 130.
+- Missing required variables -> `missing_variables` error with `missing` list.
+- Clipboard: uses platform tools via `copyToClipboard`.
+- JSON output on success: `{ success: true, id, title, characters, message }`.
+- JSON output on failure: `{ success: false, error: "clipboard_failed", fallback }`, exit 1.
+
+### render
+- Options: `--fill`, `--context <path>`, `--stdin`, `--max-context <bytes>`, `--json`.
+- Variable handling same as `copy`.
+- Context:
+  - `--stdin` reads stdin with 30s timeout and size cap (`maxContext + 1024`).
+  - `--context` reads file with size cap.
+  - If both, stdin wins.
+  - Default max context = 204800 bytes.
+  - Adds `## Context` section; adds truncation note if truncated.
+- JSON output: `{ id, title, rendered, variables?, context? }`.
+
+### export
+- Options: `--format <skill|md>` (default skill), `--output-dir <dir>` (default cwd), `--all`, `--stdout`, `--json`.
+- If not `--all` and no ids -> error exit 1.
+- If any id not found -> error exit 1.
+- `--stdout` prints content directly (no JSON summary).
+- JSON output (files): `{ success, exported: [{id,file}], failed? }`.
+
+### install
+- Options: `--project`, `--all`, `--bundle <id>`, `--force`, `--json`.
+- Target dir: project `.claude/skills` or `~/.config/claude/skills`.
+- `--bundle`:
+  - Validate bundle exists and id safe.
+  - If modified and no `--force` -> `modified_by_user` error, exit 1.
+  - Writes `SKILL.md` and updates manifest entry `kind: bundle`.
+- Prompt ids:
+  - If none -> error exit 1.
+  - Unsafe id -> error, skipped.
+  - Modified skill without `--force` -> skipped.
+  - Writes `SKILL.md` and updates manifest entry `kind: prompt`.
+- JSON output: `{ success, installed, skipped, failed, targetDir }`.
+
+### uninstall
+- Options: `--project`, `--confirm`, `--json`.
+- Validates skill IDs with `isSafeSkillId`; invalid -> exit 2.
+- Non-tty requires `--confirm` or exit 1.
+- Removes skill dir via `rmSync({ recursive: true, force: true })` and updates manifest.
+- JSON output: `{ success, removed, notFound, failed, targetDir }`.
+
+### installed
+- Options: `--personal`, `--project`, `--json`.
+- Default checks both locations.
+- JSON output: `{ installed, count, locations }`.
+
+### update
+- Options: `--personal`, `--project`, `--dry-run`, `--diff`, `--force`, `--json`.
+- Updates installed skills based on manifest.
+- Skips non JFP-generated files unless `--force`.
+- Uses `generateSkillMd` / `generateBundleSkillMd`, compares hashes.
+- `--dry-run` produces "updated" results without writing.
+- JSON output lists `updated`, `skipped`, `unchanged`, `failed` with location.
+
+### bundles / bundle
+- `bundles`: lists bundles, JSON with id/title/description/version/promptCount/featured/author.
+- `bundle <id>`: shows bundle details; JSON includes `prompts` list.
+- Not found -> `not_found` error.
+
+### suggest
+- Options: `--limit` (default 3), `--semantic`, `--json`.
+- Invalid limit -> `invalid_limit`.
+- Empty task -> `empty_task`.
+- Uses BM25 search; optional semantic rerank with `semanticRerank` (MiniLM download; fallback "hash").
+
+### interactive (i)
+- Uses `@inquirer/prompts` search UI.
+- Actions: copy, view, install (personal/project), export markdown, back, exit.
+- Install uses manifest + `generateSkillMd`.
+
+### random
+- Options: `--category`, `--tag`, `--copy`, `--json`.
+- Filter by category/tag; if none, error `no_prompts`.
+- Copy uses platform clipboard tools (xclip/xsel/pbcopy/clip).
+- Non-JSON output shows preview (first 10 lines) and metadata.
+
+### categories / tags / open / doctor / about
+- `categories`: counts prompts per category, sorted by name.
+- `tags`: counts per tag, sorted by count desc.
+- `open <id>`: opens `https://jeffreysprompts.com/prompts/<id>` via platform open command.
+- `doctor`: checks skills dirs, clipboard tool availability, bun runtime availability, registry load.
+- `about`: prints banner and dynamic stats; JSON output has counts and metadata.
+
+### status / refresh
+- `status`: shows cache status, auth status, local prompts, settings.
+- `refresh`: forces registry refresh and prints source (remote/cache/bundled).
+
+### login
+- `login` uses local browser flow unless `--remote` or no display.
+- Local flow:
+  - Opens `${JFP_PREMIUM_URL}/cli/auth?port=<port>&redirect=local`.
+  - Starts local HTTP server on 127.0.0.1; callback `/callback`.
+  - Expected query params: `token`, `email`, `tier`, `expires_at`, `user_id`, `refresh_token`.
+  - Saves credentials; renders success/error HTML pages.
+- Remote flow (device code):
+  - POST `${JFP_PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-code` with `{ client_id: "jfp-cli" }`.
+  - Poll POST `${JFP_PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-token` with `{ device_code, client_id: "jfp-cli" }`.
+  - Handles errors: `authorization_pending`, `slow_down`, `expired_token`, `access_denied`.
+
+### logout / whoami
+- `logout`: if `JFP_TOKEN` set -> error `env_token`.
+- Optional `--revoke` calls `POST /cli/revoke`.
+- `whoami`: if env token -> source environment; if expired -> `session_expired`, exit 1.
+
+### save
+- Requires auth and premium tier.
+- POST `/cli/saved-prompts` with `{ prompt_id }`.
+- If already saved (409 or code `already_saved`) -> success response with `already_saved: true`.
+
+### sync
+- Options: `--force`, `--status`, `--json`.
+- `--status` prints sync metadata and auth status.
+- GET `/cli/sync?since=<lastSync>` unless `--force`.
+- Writes `prompts.json` and `sync.meta.json` (version `1.0.0`).
+
+### notes
+- GET `/cli/notes/<promptId>` to list.
+- POST `/cli/notes/<promptId>` with `{ content }` to add.
+- DELETE `/cli/notes/<promptId>/<noteId>` to delete.
+- Requires auth and premium.
+
+### collections
+- Endpoints:
+  - GET `/cli/collections` (list)
+  - GET `/cli/collections/<name>` (detail)
+  - POST `/cli/collections` (create)
+  - POST `/cli/collections/<name>/prompts` (add)
+- `collections export`:
+  - Resolves prompts from local registry, then offline library, then `/cli/prompts/<id>`.
+  - Exports skill or md to stdout or files.
+
+### skills
+- Endpoints:
+  - GET `/cli/skills` (list)
+  - POST `/cli/skills/<id>/install`
+  - GET `/cli/skills/<id>/export`
+- `skills create` scaffolds SKILL.md template in `.claude/skills/<name>` by default.
+
+### completion
+- Generates shell completions for bash, zsh, fish.
+- Detects shell from `SHELL` env if `--shell` not provided.
+
+### serve
+- MCP server using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` with stdio transport.
+- Resources: `prompt://<id>` returns prompt content.
+- Tools:
+  - `search_prompts` (query/category/tags/limit)
+  - `render_prompt` (id, variables, context)
+
+### update-cli
+- Checks latest GitHub release and updates binary.
+- Hidden command `update-check-internal` updates cached version info.
+- Uses SHA256 checksum validation.
+
+### help
+- Prints structured help; JSON uses `getHelpData()`.
+
+## 11) Auto-Update Check
+File: `packages/cli/src/lib/auto-update.ts`
+- Checks GitHub releases once per day.
+- If cached update exists, prints banner (skips if `--json` or `serve`).
+- Spawns detached `update-check-internal` to refresh cache.
+
+## 12) Known Quirks
+- `searchPersonal` calls `response.json()` on ApiResponse; as written, this will throw (bug). Document for parity before fixing.

+## Core Dependencies (beyond /dp)
+- CLI: `clap` (derive), `clap_complete`.
+- Serialization: `serde`, `serde_json`, `serde_yaml`.

+- SQLite: `sqlmodel-sqlite` (via /dp/sqlmodel_rust) + `rusqlite` under the hood.
+- Locking: `fs4` for cross-process locks.
+- Hashing: `sha2`, `hex`.
+- Time: `chrono` or `time`.
+- Temp files: `tempfile`.

+## Sync + Recovery
+- One-way sync: SQLite -> JSONL by default.
+- Lock file: `~/.config/jfp/locks/sync.lock`.
+- Atomic JSONL write via temp file + fsync + rename.
+- Recovery commands:
+  - `jfp export-jsonl`
+  - `jfp import-jsonl`
+  - `jfp db check`
